title: At his OpenAI trial, Musk relitigates an old friendship
description: It's a story Musk has told before -- in interviews and to author Walter Isaacson for his bestselling biography of Musk -- but Tuesday was the first time he said it under oath.

title: OpenAI Really Wants Codex to Shut Up About Goblins
description: “Never talk about goblins, gremlins, raccoons, trolls, ogres, pigeons, or other animals or creatures unless it is absolutely and unambiguously relevant,” reads OpenAI’s coding agent instructions.

title: Anti-Trump Instagram pic of seashells now enough to indict ex-FBI directors
description: The clown car is all gassed up.

title: Elon Musk Testifies That He Started OpenAI to Prevent a ‘Terminator Outcome’
description: The judge also warned Musk and Sam Altman to curb their “propensity to use social media to make things worse outside the courtroom” after both sides traded attacks online.

title: Flesh-eating bacteria devour man's arm and leg in just three days
description: When doctors saw him, his limbs were discolored and crackling.

title: FCC orders review of ABC licenses after Kimmel joke offends Trump and first lady
description: Kimmel joke calling Melania an "expectant widow" followed quickly by FCC order.

title: Amazon is already offering new OpenAI products on AWS
description: A day after OpenAI got Microsoft to agree to end exclusive rights, AWS announced a slate of OpenAI model offerings, including a new agent service.

title: Drone pilot makes US rescind no-fly zones around unmarked, moving ICE vehicles
description: Civil liberty concerns spur FAA to revise drone no-fly zones near ICE vehicles.

title: Amazon launches an AI-powered audio Q&A experience on product pages
description: Amazon's new "Join the chat" feature lets you ask questions about products and receive AI-powered audio responses.

title: ‘It’s Undignified’: Hundreds of Workers Training Meta’s AI Could Be Laid Off
description: More than 700 people working for a Meta contractor in Ireland are at risk of losing their jobs, documents show.

title: Google expands Pentagon’s access to its AI after Anthropic’s refusal
description: After Anthropic refused to allow the DoD to use its AI for domestic mass surveillance and autonomous weapons, Google has signed a new contract with the department.

title: Humanoid robots start sorting luggage in Tokyo airport test amid labor shortage
description: Humanoid robots could load cargo and clean aircraft cabins at Haneda Airport.

title: Lovable launches its vibe-coding app on iOS and Android
description: The app allows developers to vibe code web apps and websites on the go.

title: The Race Is on to Keep AI Agents From Running Wild With Your Credit Cards
description: AI agents may soon be buying your stuff for you. The FIDO Alliance has teamed up with Google and Mastercard to try to ensure that shopping in the near future isn't a complete disaster.

title: The Bloomberg Terminal Is Getting an AI Makeover, Like It or Not
description: WIRED spoke with Bloomberg’s chief technology officer about the big, chatbot-style changes coming to the iconic platform for traders.

title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman are going to court over OpenAI’s future
description: After a yearslong legal feud, Elon Musk and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman are heading to trial this week in Northern California in a case that could have sweeping consequences. title: Distill Hiatus
description: After five years, Distill will be taking a break.

title: Weight Banding
